title: Hypothesis-Testing
text: estimate Bayes factors single parameter, set parameters, can use bf_table() function. Bayes factors computed via Savage–Dickey density ratio (SDDR; Wagenmakers et al., 2010), , point null H0:θ=θ0H_0 : \theta = \theta_0 nested H1:θ≠θ0H_1 : \theta \neq \theta_0 shared nuisance parameters, gives BF01=p(θ=θ0∣y)p(θ=θ0), \mathrm{BF}_{01} \;=\; \frac{p(\theta = \theta_0 \mid y)}{p(\theta = \theta_0)}, .e. ratio posterior prior density evaluated null value. bf_table() reports BF10=1/BF01\mathrm{BF}_{10} = 1 / \mathrm{BF}_{01} approximating posterior density θ0=0\theta_0 = 0 MCMC draws. perform hypothesis test effects involving specific variable, can call bf_table() function, specifying variable want look using variable argument. Lets investigate hours sleep individual got previous night (sleep_hour) influence network structure: output now shows 6 rows. five “Fixed Effect” rows show covariate sleep_hour influence node separately. “Fixed Effect (join)” row displays single Bayes factor case tests hypothesis H: difference baseline network structure explained variable sleep_hour can see, BF_{10} = bf_tab[]. Therefore can conlude variable sleep_hour influence baseline level network.

title: Missing-Data
text: Currently, bvarnet handles missing data listwise deletion skipping lag-mechanism. Listwise deletion computational simple solution missing data problem, rely assumption data missing completely random (MCAR). Violating assumption may lead biased parameter estimation. longitudinal settings like Vector Autoregressive models, listwise deletion can introduce irregular gaps observed time series. violates modeling assumption missing time points equal length time gaps. address issue, introduce skipping-lag mechanism. Specifically, lagged effects estimated time difference two observations exceed threshold determined autoregressive order K. , VAR(K) model, lagged relationships included effective time gap corresponds K time steps. words, model excludes estimation temporal parameters across large time gaps, thereby preserving interpretation lagged effects local (short-term) dependencies.

what: Comparison: With and Without Lag Skipping
title: Missing-Data
text: bvar function skipping-lag mechanism can turned using skip_lag argument. illustrate , can fit model using lag skipping mechanism, without:
code: fit_no_skip_lag <- bvar(   id_col = "id",   time_col = "day",   y_cols = c("anxious", "calm", "conventional", "critical", "dependable"),   x_cols = NULL,   re_temporal = FALSE,   K = 1,   skip_lag = FALSE,   data = studentlife,   family = c("ordinal"),   priors = set_priors(),   iter = 4000,   warmup = 1000,   chains = 4,   cores = 4,   seed = 1337 )  fit_skip_lag <- bvar(   id_col = "id",   time_col = "day",   y_cols = c("anxious", "calm", "conventional", "critical", "dependable"),   x_cols = NULL,   re_temporal = FALSE,   K = 1,   skip_lag = TRUE,   data = studentlife,   family = c("ordinal"),   priors = set_priors(),   iter = 4000,   warmup = 1000,   chains = 4,   cores = 4,   seed = 1337 )

what: Why does skip_lag = FALSE produce fewer observations?
title: Missing-Data
text: skip_lag = FALSE, row whose previous observation exactly one time step away lacks valid lag. autoregressive component computed row, row dropped entirely likelihood. conservative choice: every observation remains genuine lagged value, estimates temporal coefficients (ϕ\phi) unbiased (given model). skip_lag = TRUE (default), rows kept. contribution autoregressive effect set zero, outcome still modeled intercept, fixed effects, (present) random effects. Oly temporal component “switched ” row.

title: Missing-Data
text: two approaches trade sample size potential bias autoregressive estimates: skip_lag = FALSE — Every row valid lag, ϕ\phi estimates reflect true short-term dynamics. However, datasets many irregular gaps (present one), large share observations discarded, reducing statistical power parameters. skip_lag = TRUE — observations contribute estimation intercept, fixed effects, residual variance. zero-filled lags treated “temporal information” rather “lag zero”, rows gaps gently pull ϕ\phi toward zero. datasets many gaps can lead slight weakening autoregressive estimates. Currently, sophisticated missing data handling (e.g. imputation) implemented users .

text: object bvarnet object returned bvar(). type Character vector "" (default).  parameter groups test. Options: "ar" (autoregressive), "cl" (cross-lagged), "intercepts", "fe" (non-intercept fixed effects), "lag_fe" (lag × predictor interaction joint tests), "temporal" (joint test phi parameters across lags, .e. entire temporal structure AR + CL, excluding covariates; additionally emits separate joint rows AR-CL-components; lag × covariate interactions present, additional rows emitted per-interaction-term AR-/ CL-interaction sub-tests, plus full temporal + interactions omnibus). "" auto-selects applicable types (skips "intercepts" ordinal models "lag_fe" lag interactions exist).  variable set, "" also skips "intercepts" "fe". Per-cell "ar" "cl" rows respect lag argument; "temporal" always covers lags via joint tests. lag Integer; lag block use (default 1). Applies "ar" "cl" types. null_value Numeric scalar; null hypothesis value (default 0). variable Character vector NULL (default).  One variable names — either network variables (colnames(standata$Y)) covariates (x_cols, excluding "Intercept" interaction columns).  set, effects involving variables included: network variables filter phi rows (effects variable lagged predictor); covariate names filter fixed-effect rows lag × covariate interaction rows (effects covariate). types can combined single call. combined type = "intercepts".

code: bvar(   id_col,   time_col,   y_cols,   x_cols = NULL,   center_x = FALSE,   fe_interactions = NULL,   re_interactions = NULL,   re_cols = NULL,   re_temporal = FALSE,   K = 1,   na_action = c("listwise"),   skip_lag = TRUE,   data,   family = c("bernoulli", "ordinal", "gaussian"),   priors = set_priors(),   iter = 4000,   warmup = 1000,   chains = 4,   cores = 1,   seed = NULL,   adapt_delta = NULL,   max_treedepth = NULL,   save_data = FALSE )

title: Fit a Bayesian multilevel VAR network model — bvar
text: id_col Character. Name subject/group identifier column. time_col Character. Name time column. y_cols Character vector. Names outcome columns. x_cols Character vector NULL. Names covariate columns. center_x Logical. Grand-mean centre covariates fitting? Default FALSE. fe_interactions List NULL. Fixed-effect interaction terms add design matrix. element character vector column names interact, c("lag", "x") interact lag columns covariate. re_interactions List NULL. Random-effect interaction terms. re_cols Character vector. Columns X /"Intercept" include random slopes. re_temporal Logical. Include random slopes lag predictors? Default FALSE. K Integer. AR order. Default 1. na_action Character. Missing-data strategy; currently "listwise". skip_lag Logical. TRUE (default), rows irregular time gaps lag set zero rather dropped. data Data frame long format. family Character scalar vector. Observation model per node. scalar recycled y_cols. vector length length(y_cols) (named positional) specifies per-node families. Valid values: "bernoulli", "ordinal", "gaussian". priors bvarnet_priors object set_priors(). Defaults set_priors() (package defaults). iter Integer. Number post-warmup iterations per chain. Default 4000. warmup Integer. Number warmup iterations per chain. Default 1000. chains Integer. Number MCMC chains. Default 4. cores Integer. Number chains run parallel. Default 1. seed Integer NULL. RNG seed. adapt_delta Numeric (0, 1). Target average proposal acceptance probability warmup adaptation. Higher values (e.g., 0.95–0.99) reduce divergences cost slower sampling. Default NULL (CmdStan default 0.8). max_treedepth Integer. Maximum depth NUTS binary tree. Increasing allows sampler take leapfrog steps per iteration, can help difficult posteriors (e.g., funnels hierarchical logistic models) increases computation. Default NULL (CmdStan default 10). save_data Logical. TRUE, store preprocessed (sorted, listwise-deleted) estimation data data_used slot returned object reproducibility downstream analyses. Default FALSE.

title: Fit a Bayesian multilevel VAR network model — bvar
text: bvarnet object (named list) slots: draws, convergence, diagnostics, timing, metadata, return_codes, family, standata, priors. save_data = TRUE, also includes data_used (cleaned estimation data frame).
